AMR Operations · Inbound Parcel
Onboarding & Training Plan
Onboarding & Training
 Apple Logistics

Inbound Parcel Network

AMR Logistics Operations
The Parcel RSM Mission

The Inbound Parcel RSM ensures carrier execution from OEM handover through delivery by monitoring network health, aligning capacity with forecast, and resolving operational exceptions across the AMR region.

30-Day Roadmap Daily Workflow Action Triggers Training Phases TMS Deep Dive Carrier Reference Geography Cadence Resources

Training Phases

Countries

Gateways

OEMs

Carrier Partners
First 30 Days Roadmap
A week-by-week progression from orientation through independent operations.
Week 1
Foundations
  • Geographic overview (4 countries, 9 gateways)
  • Carrier landscape (UPS, FedEx, DHL)
  • Dashboards & SmartPack orientation
  • OEM overview & Greenhouse access
  • Hub locations & carrier routing basics
Week 2
Daily Operations
  • OTG review & At Risk identification
  • In Network monitoring
  • Handover & Uplift tracking
  • Forecast vs Actuals & CPT Pivot
  • FedEx OTG portal validation
Week 3
Weekly Cadence
  • UPS Monday capacity request
  • Friday TMS settings (shadow session)
  • RRG navigation & version control
  • Forecasting & carrier selection logic
  • IB/OB Synch call participation
Week 4
Advanced & Independent
  • NPI planning & launch support
  • Exception handling & escalations
  • Routing changes & RRG ad hoc
  • Own a Friday TMS setup (supervised)
  • Open POD reconciliation
Typical Daily Workflow
The RSM's day is rhythm-driven. Follow this chronological flow from first check to final handover.
Morning
  1. OTG review — identify At Risk volume by carrier
  2. In Network review — check hub departure status
  3. Handover check — confirm OEM-to-carrier handover
  4. Forecast vs Actuals — surface gaps early
Midday
  1. Carrier follow-ups — UPS/FedEx/DHL action items
  2. OEM exception triage — equipment or lane issues
  3. Open POD check — verify pending deliveries
Afternoon
  1. Escalation management — flag unresolved risk
  2. Prepare daily handover notes
  3. Pre-position for next-day operations
What Triggers Action
Know the signals. When you see these conditions, take the corresponding action immediately.
Trigger Action Urgency
OTG volume increasing Contact origin carrier team — investigate uplift delays High
In Network volume stuck at hub Check flight departure schedule — verify carrier movement plan High
Forecast vs Actuals gap widening Investigate OEM handover — compare planned vs actual handover volume Medium
EDD shift detected Escalate to carrier — confirm revised delivery commitment High
UPS capacity constraint (post-Monday) Initiate mid-week TMS adjustment — consider FedEx/DHL reroute Medium
FedEx pushback on ad hoc request Engage FedEx key contact (Ashleigh) — escalate to manager if needed Medium
Dead freight event Document occurrence — engage carrier for root cause and resolution Standard
Open POD aging beyond threshold Alert stakeholders — initiate carrier investigation Medium
NPI volume surge incoming Pre-book UPS capacity — confirm TMS routing supports launch plan High
RRG mismatch with TMS settings Initiate RRG amendment — do not proceed until codes align High
Training Phases
Progress through each phase sequentially. Links open relevant dashboards, tools, and references.
1

Week 1 — Geographic overview, carrier landscape, and key tooling

Geographic Overview
4 countries (CN, VN, IN, TH), 9 gateways, 40 OEMs. Understand origin structures and carrier routing by region.
Foundation

Carrier Overview

Foundation
Parcel Dashboard

The living day-to-day navigation guide for the Parcel RSM role. Your primary reference document.

Greenhouse OEM Capability Dashboard

Orientation to the IB Parcel Dashboard and QBR Dashboard — primary monitoring surfaces for the role.

Parcel Dashboard Parcel QBR Dashboard
2

Daily Operations

Week 2 — On The Ground, In Network, Handover, and daily reporting

In Network Overview
Volume that has uplifted from origin but not yet departed the carrier's domestic hub. Learn to interpret status and identify risk.
Daily Ops
Parcel In Network Report
On The Ground (OTG)
Volume handed over from OEM to carrier but not yet uplifted. Learn At Risk vs Not At Risk, tonnage/ship/unit nuances, and carrier-specific OTG behaviors (UPS rolls WoW; FedEx/DHL typically do not).
Daily Ops
On The Ground Report
Handover & Uplift
Understand the relationship between Handover, Uplift, and OTG. Review the Handover report and Handover Tracking tool.
Daily Ops
Parcel Handover
Daily IB Review
Structure of the daily inbound review — what triggers action, how to prioritise Express vs Standard, and AOS by Account # (FIFO logic).
Daily Ops
Daily Handover Process
How volume is formally handed from OEM to carrier. Understand the downstream impact on Uplift and OTG reporting.
Daily Ops
Forecast vs Actuals
Review the Forecast vs Actuals report and CPT Pivot. Understand how to read gaps and surface risk early.
Daily Ops
LBR Flow Capacities
FedEx OTG Portal
For FedEx shipments, pull OTG tracking numbers and review directly on the FedEx portal to verify status.
Carrier Tool
FedEx Tracking Portal
Dead Freight
Understanding dead freight — what it is, when it occurs, and how to manage it with carriers.
Ad Hoc
3

Weekly Operations & Carrier Management

Week 3 — Capacity requests, TMS settings, RRG, and carrier-specific workflows

UPS Monday Capacity Request
Weekly capacity request process for UPS — the Monday file. Walk through the setup, automation tools, and how to submit accurately.
Weekly
UPS Capacity Automation
Overview of automation tooling for UPS capacity. RTL Parcel Projections and how to manage UPS IB/OB Parcel Synch calls.
Weekly
Friday TMS Settings
End-of-week TMS configuration — how to set up for the following week's routing and carrier assignments.
Weekly
🔍 Deep Dive: TMS (Transportation Management System)
A dedicated session covering the end-to-end TMS workflow for the IB Parcel RSM role. See full breakdown below.
Deep DiveWeekly
Regional Routing Guide (RRG)
Navigate the RRG — Gateway Matrix, Parcel Account Numbers, Direct Ship POE Table, and Carrier Codes for EDI 856. Understand version control practices.
Weekly
Parcel Account Numbers & ARK
How parcel account numbers are structured and managed via the Account Request Keeper (ARK).
Weekly
Carrier Responsibilities: Handover to POD
Full carrier accountability model from handover through to Proof of Delivery. Understand the RSM role at each stage.
Weekly
Forecasting
How parcel volume is forecast, and the RSM's role in validating and acting on forecast data.
Weekly
How Carriers Are Chosen
The decision logic behind carrier selection — by country, package size, OEM constraints, and cost considerations.
Foundation
4

Advanced Topics & Ad Hoc Operations

Week 4+ — NPI, RRGs, new carrier onboarding, and escalation management

NPI Planning
New Product Introduction planning for parcel — carrier selection, capacity pre-booking, and the RSM's role in supporting launch execution. UPS is sole NPI carrier.
NPI
RRG Deep Dive
Advanced walk-through of the Regional Routing Guide. Gateway Matrix, carrier code assignments, and edge cases.
Advanced
New Carrier Onboarding (OEM Support)
Who owns new carrier onboarding for OEMs and how the OB RSM supports IB requirements in that process.
Advanced
RRG Ad Hoc Requests
How to handle ad hoc routing requests — when they arise, who to engage, and how to document them.
Ad Hoc
Open POD Reconciliation
Verifying and actioning all open Proof of Delivery items. Escalation paths and carrier engagement protocols.
Ad Hoc
Team Structure & RSM Split
New team structure — UPS requires 2 RSMs split by execution. Understand remit boundaries and collaboration model.
Advanced
🔍 Deep Dive: TMS (Transportation Management System)
A structured session to build full working proficiency with the TMS in the IB Parcel RSM context. Complete after Week 3 cadence sessions.
A

What Is TMS & Why It Matters

Orientation — purpose, scope, and the RSM's role in TMS

TMS Overview
The Transportation Management System controls routing rules, carrier assignments, and shipment planning for the week ahead. For the IB Parcel RSM, the most critical TMS touchpoint is the Friday Settings — configuring routing for the following week before the Monday capacity request cycle begins.
Foundation
TMS vs. Dashboard Relationship
The TMS is the instruction layer — it tells carriers what to expect and sets routing rules. The Parcel Dashboard and OTG/In Network reports are the visibility layer — showing you whether execution matches the plan. Mismatches between the two are your signal to escalate or adjust.
Foundation
RSM TMS Responsibilities
The RSM owns: (1) Friday weekly setup for the coming week; (2) Mid-week adjustments for exceptions or volume changes; (3) Coordination with OEMs and carriers to ensure TMS settings reflect actual handover plans; (4) Validation that EDI 856 carrier codes are correctly mapped in the RRG.
Foundation
B

Friday TMS Settings — Step by Step

The weekly configuration workflow every RSM must master

Step 1 — Review Forecast vs. Actuals
Before touching TMS, pull the Forecast vs. Actuals report and CPT Pivot. Understand the volume delta between what was planned and what actually moved. This informs your routing decisions for the following week.
Friday Workflow
Step 2 — Check OTG & In Network Status
Before locking in next week's settings, confirm the current week's OTG volume disposition. Any volume rolling over from the current week (particularly UPS) affects your starting point for next week's settings.
Friday Workflow
Step 3 — Configure Carrier Assignments
Set the carrier routing for each OEM/origin lane for the upcoming week. Key rules: (1) US — all 3 carriers possible; (2) CA — UPS primary, FedEx for AirTags; (3) MX — FedEx sole carrier, UPS does not service MX; (4) CL — DHL primary. Confirm no capacity constraints from Monday's UPS request that require rerouting.
Friday Workflow
Step 4 — Validate RRG Alignment
Cross-reference TMS settings against the RRG Gateway Matrix and Parcel Account Numbers tabs. Ensure the carrier codes entered match the EDI 856 codes in the RRG. Mismatches here cause downstream visibility failures.
Friday Workflow
OEM-Specific Routing Confirmations
Confirm TMS settings reflect actual OEM capabilities before locking in. Trust but verify — don't rely solely on TMS defaults.
Friday Workflow
Step 6 — Document & Hand Off
Log the TMS settings snapshot before leaving for the weekend. Ensure any exceptions or non-standard configurations are noted in the daily handover for Monday visibility. If a synch call or coverage is in place over the weekend, communicate open items.
Friday Workflow
C

TMS & the UPS Monday Capacity Cycle

How TMS settings feed into the weekly UPS capacity request

The Friday → Monday Connection
The TMS settings you lock in on Friday directly inform the UPS Monday capacity request. The capacity numbers you submit Monday morning are based on the routing volume you've configured in TMS for each origin/lane. If TMS is wrong, the capacity ask is wrong.
Cycle
UPS Automation Tool
The UPS capacity automation tool reads from or feeds into TMS settings. Understand how these two systems interact — specifically, whether automation handles lane-level routing or whether manual overrides in TMS are required for exceptions.
Cycle
IB/OB Synch Impact
The Monday UPS IB/OB Parcel Synch call may trigger TMS mid-week adjustments. If OB volumes shift significantly, the RSM may need to update TMS routing mid-week to rebalance capacity. Always check whether a synch call outcome requires a TMS update before the next Friday cycle.
Cycle
D

TMS Exception Handling & Ad Hoc Adjustments

When and how to make mid-week changes

When to Adjust TMS Mid-Week
Mid-week TMS changes are triggered by: (1) carrier capacity constraints identified post-Monday; (2) OEM equipment or lane constraints (e.g., V158, CGO); (3) NPI surge requiring rerouting; (4) Dead freight events; (5) Carrier escalations (e.g., FedEx pushback on ad hoc requests).
Ad Hoc
FedEx-Specific TMS Caution
FedEx does not have dynamic EDDs. If a TMS change requires FedEx to adjust expected delivery dates, expect pushback and slow response times. Build extra lead time into any FedEx TMS adjustments vs. UPS.
Ad Hoc
RRG Version Control During Changes
Any ad hoc TMS change that introduces a new lane or carrier code must be verified against the current RRG version. If the RRG doesn't reflect the change, initiate an RRG amendment as part of the ad hoc process.
Ad Hoc
E

TMS Practice Exercise

Hands-on validation before going independent

Shadow a Full Friday TMS Setup
Before going independent: shadow a full Friday TMS session end-to-end, including the Forecast vs. Actuals review, OTG check, carrier assignment configuration, RRG validation, and handover note. Ask questions at each step.
Practice
Own a Friday TMS Setup (Supervised)
Run the Friday TMS setup independently with a senior RSM available for review and sign-off before the settings are confirmed. Repeat until confident.
Practice
Walk Through a Historical Exception
Review a past mid-week TMS adjustment — ideally one triggered by a carrier escalation or capacity crunch. Understand what changed, why, and how it was resolved. Common examples: FedEx MX pushback on ad hoc requests; CL DHL volume reroute to FedEx backup.
Practice
Carrier Reference
Key characteristics and usage guidelines for each carrier partner.
UPS
Founded 1907 · Louisville, KY
Hub: SDF — Louisville, KY
Primary Carrier · Sole NPI
  • Best OTP and overall processes
  • Superior NPI performance in US & CA
  • Most expensive for small packages; savings on large packages
  • Easiest to work with for ad hoc solutions
  • UPS brokerage used for DHL customs clearance
  • Largest carrier — requires 2 RSMs split by execution
🇺🇸 US 🇨🇦 CA 🇲🇽 MX — not serviced
FedEx
Founded 1971 · Memphis, TN
Hub: MEM — Memphis, TN
Reliable Carrier · Backup NPI
  • Cheapest for small packages; costly over weekly allotments
  • Difficult to work with for ad hoc or out-of-plan requests
  • Best option for MX BAU and NPI
  • Toluca (MX) location well-managed — govt/military airport
  • Currently sole MX carrier — UPS does not service MX
  • Key contact: Ashleigh and her manager
  • Already onboarded to CL as secondary option
🇺🇸 US 🇲🇽 MX (primary) 🇨🇱 CL (secondary)
DHL
Sub of Deutsche Post · Founded 1969
Hub: CVG (Cincinnati) · LAX
Regional Carrier · Sole CL
  • Cheapest across almost all locations and package sizes
  • Worst OTP and support performance overall
  • Customs clearance issues (via UPS brokerage)
  • Difficult to resolve issues when they arise
  • Currently only Chile (CL) provider onboarded
🇨🇱 CL (sole carrier)
Carrier Usage by Country
Understand which carriers operate where and any origin/OEM constraints.
🇺🇸
United States
UPS FedEx DHL

All 3 carriers active

🇨🇦
Canada
UPS

UPS only (except AirTags — FedEx small weight cheaper). DHL not set up.

🇲🇽
Mexico
FedEx

FedEx is the sole carrier. UPS does not service MX.

🇨🇱
Chile
DHL FedEx

DHL sole primary carrier. FedEx onboarded as secondary option.

Operational Cadence
Key recurring tasks and when they happen.
Task Frequency Day / Timing Notes
IB Parcel Daily Review (On The Ground + In Network) Daily Every day Review OTG, In Network, and Handover. Identify At Risk volume.
Daily Handover Daily Every day Volume handover from OEM to carrier. Confirm uplift plans with UPS APAC team.
Open POD Reconciliation Daily Every day Verify and action all open PODs. Alert stakeholders of discrepancies.
UPS Capacity Request (Monday File) Weekly Monday Submit capacity request for the coming week. Use automation tooling where available.
UPS IB/OB Parcel Synch Call Weekly Monday Align IB and OB teams with UPS on volume and capacity.
Friday TMS Settings Weekly Friday Configure TMS for the following week's routing and carrier assignments.
Forecast vs Actuals Review Weekly Weekly Review forecast accuracy. Surface gaps and escalate risk.
RRG Ad Hoc Requests Ad Hoc As needed Handle out-of-plan routing requests. Engage carriers and document decisions.
NPI Planning Ad Hoc Per launch cycle Pre-book capacity, manage uplift, and support launch execution with UPS.
Dead Freight Discussion Ad Hoc As needed Review dead freight occurrences and resolution with carriers.
